app.name: AiOverviewControl
app.title: AI Usage Control
card.provider_description: Choose a provider supported by local helpers or fallback.
error.api_mode: API mode needs provider API tokens or native provider keys.
error.helper_missing: Local provider helper is missing or not executable: {path}
error.helper_timeout: Provider helper timed out while fetching usage data.
error.oauth_mode: OAuth mode requires provider authentication supported by the selected provider.
error.web_mode: Web mode depends on the optional fallback provider implementation.
settings.auth.codexbar_1: codex, gemini, perplexity, cursor, cline, opencode, kilo, kiro, warp, amp
settings.auth.codexbar_2: Requires codexbar installed and configured with provider credentials
settings.auth.copilot_1: Priority: gh auth token → COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN → GH_TOKEN → GITHUB_TOKEN
settings.auth_ref_desc: How each provider type obtains credentials.
settings.codexbar_path_desc: Absolute path to the codexbar executable. Only required for providers without a native adapter. Leave empty to auto-detect in PATH, ~/.local/bin, and /usr/local/bin.
settings.codexbar_providers_desc: Require codexbar installed. Without it, they appear as errors.
settings.diagnostics_desc: Commands to validate the pipeline outside the widget.
settings.footer: Native adapters (Claude, Copilot, DeepSeek, Kimi, GLM, MiniMax, Cloudflare, Ollama, Vertex AI) work without codexbar. Mistral, NVIDIA, Qwen, and BytePlus validate the key but have no quota endpoint — they display an informational card.
settings.hero.description: Monitor usage quotas for {count} AI providers directly in the DankBar. Providers are queried independently — one failure does not affect the others.
settings.language.description: UI language for this plugin. Auto follows system locale.
settings.language.label: Language
settings.native_providers_desc: These providers use local scripts or direct APIs — they work without codexbar. Click to enable/disable.
settings.refresh.120000: 2 minutes — recommended default for normal use.
settings.refresh.300000: 5 minutes — suitable for many providers or slow networks.
settings.refresh.60000: 1 minute — active monitoring. Increases API calls.
settings.refresh.900000: 15 minutes — conservative, reduces rate limits on external APIs.
settings.refresh_description: How often local scripts fetch data from each provider.
settings.required_env: Environment variables required for active providers:
settings.show_errors_desc: Keeps error cards visible in the panel. Recommended while configuring new providers to identify authentication failures.
settings.source.api: api — uses API tokens configured in codexbar.
settings.source.auto: auto — lets codexbar choose the source per provider.
settings.source.cli: cli — best default on Linux. Local telemetry, zero network calls.
settings.source.oauth: oauth — authenticates via OAuth where the provider supports it.
settings.source.web: web — dashboard scraping. May be macOS-only for some providers.
settings.source_mode_desc: Passed to codexbar for providers that use it. Native adapters ignore this value.
status.fetching: Fetching usage windows from local provider helpers.
status.no_data_hint: Run your configured AI CLIs and refresh to populate usage windows.
status.no_provider_data: No provider data available. Check credentials, local CLIs, or the optional fallback path.
error.fetch_failed: Failed to fetch usage from providers.
error.parse_failed: Failed to parse provider helper output.
error.claude_unavailable: Claude Code usage details are unavailable. Check claude, jq, and curl.
error.claude_timeout: Claude Code usage fetch timed out.
